vocabulary.meaning: to dress yourself in something
vocabulary.example: He put on his coat and walked out.

vocabulary.meaning: to have clothing, jewelry, etc. on your body
vocabulary.example: She was wearing a beautiful red dress.

vocabulary.meaning: a set of clothes worn for a particular activity or by an actor
vocabulary.example: The actors wore traditional costumes for the play.

vocabulary.meaning: a set of clothes made of the same fabric, typically consisting of a jacket and trousers or a skirt
vocabulary.example: He wore a smart dark suit to the job interview.

vocabulary.meaning: to change the color of something, especially by using a special liquid
vocabulary.example: She decided to dye her hair blonde.

vocabulary.meaning: to cover a surface with colored liquid to decorate or protect it
vocabulary.example: We need to paint the front door.

vocabulary.meaning: to be the right size or shape for someone or something
vocabulary.example: These shoes don't fit me very well.

vocabulary.meaning: to look good on someone, or to be convenient/acceptable
vocabulary.example: That haircut really suits you.

vocabulary.meaning: if two things match, they look attractive together because they are similar or share a color/pattern
vocabulary.example: Your tie doesn't match your shirt.

vocabulary.meaning: material made by weaving cotton, wool, etc., used for making clothes
vocabulary.example: She bought some green cloth to make a skirt.

vocabulary.meaning: clothes, especially a particular type of clothes
vocabulary.example: You should wear warm clothing in the winter.

vocabulary.meaning: a shirt for women, typically loose-fitting and lightweight
vocabulary.example: She wore a white silk blouse with her suit.

vocabulary.meaning: a piece of clothing worn on the upper part of the body
vocabulary.example: She bought a new top to go with her jeans.

vocabulary.meaning: to decide how something will look, be made, etc., by drawing plans for it
vocabulary.example: She designs and makes her own clothes.

vocabulary.meaning: to make goods in large quantities, especially using machinery
vocabulary.example: The company manufactures clothing for children.

vocabulary.meaning: happening or existing now; of the present time
vocabulary.example: Who is the current director of the design agency?

vocabulary.meaning: recently made, built, or bought
vocabulary.example: I bought a new pair of shoes yesterday.

vocabulary.meaning: relating to the present time or recent times
vocabulary.example: Modern architecture often uses glass and steel.

vocabulary.meaning: the appearance of someone or something
vocabulary.example: The actress wore a retro look at the awards show.

vocabulary.meaning: the way that someone or something looks
vocabulary.example: She is very concerned about her physical appearance.

vocabulary.meaning: to provide something that is needed or wanted
vocabulary.example: The factory supplies clothing to stores across the country.

vocabulary.meaning: to make, write, or create something
vocabulary.example: The region produces high-quality silk.

vocabulary.meaning: to see someone or something for a very short time and not clearly
vocabulary.example: I glimpsed a famous model backstage.

vocabulary.meaning: to look quickly at someone or something
vocabulary.example: She glanced at her watch to check the time.

vocabulary.meaning: typical, normal, or in the middle of a range
vocabulary.example: The average height for men in the country is 1.75 meters.

vocabulary.meaning: ordinary, typical, or happening every day
vocabulary.example: These shoes are not suitable for everyday wear.

vocabulary.meaning: too proud of your own appearance, abilities, or achievements
vocabulary.example: He is so vain that he cannot pass a mirror without looking in it.

vocabulary.meaning: a woolen sweater worn on the upper body
vocabulary.example: She wore a thick woolly jumper to stay warm.

vocabulary.meaning: shoes with heels that are raised high off the ground
vocabulary.example: She struggled to walk in her high heels.

vocabulary.meaning: the feeling of being annoyed or disappointed because you cannot achieve what you want
vocabulary.example: He shook his head in frustration when he couldn't solve the puzzle.

vocabulary.meaning: to force yourself to be brave enough to do something difficult or dangerous
vocabulary.example: He plucked up the courage to ask her out.

vocabulary.meaning: a square board divided into 64 smaller black and white squares, used for playing chess
vocabulary.example: We set up the pieces on the chess board.

vocabulary.meaning: making you believe something that is not true
vocabulary.example: The sunny weather was deceptive, as it was actually very cold outside.

vocabulary.meaning: shocking, morally unacceptable, or extremely unusual
vocabulary.example: The cost of the designer handbag was outrageous.

vocabulary.meaning: to say or do something that is rude and offensive to someone
vocabulary.example: He insulted her fashion sense.

vocabulary.meaning: the qualities that make a person or thing different from others
vocabulary.example: The designer encourages people to express their individuality.

vocabulary.meaning: a piece of jewelry worn around the neck
vocabulary.example: She wore a beautiful pearl necklace.

vocabulary.meaning: a cloth that is slightly wet, used for cleaning
vocabulary.example: Wipe the surface with a damp cloth.

vocabulary.meaning: a clip used for fastening clothes to a clothesline
vocabulary.example: She used clothes pegs to hang the laundry on the line.

vocabulary.meaning: a small shop that sells fashionable clothes or expensive presents
vocabulary.example: She bought her wedding dress from a boutique.

phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Sports shoes catch on very quickly among teenagers.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: get rid of or abolish something
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The school decided to do away with uniforms.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: prepare a plan, list, or official document
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The lawyer drew up a contract for the new collection.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: put on formal clothes or fancy dress
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Kids love to dress up for Halloween.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: repeat, study, or examine something carefully
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Let's go over the design designs again.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: become too big for a garment, or develop from
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He grew out of his winter coat in just six months.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: pass knowledge or possessions to a younger generation
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: This watch was handed down from my grandfather.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: form or stand in a queue
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: People lined up to buy the new designer sneakers.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: visit someone or somewhere quickly or briefly
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I'll pop into the boutique on my way home.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: take someone on a tour of a place
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The manager showed us around the fashion studio.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: remove a piece of clothing; leave the ground
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He took off his wet boots at the door.
EX: She tore up the letter in anger.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: put on clothing to see if it fits or looks good
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: You should try on the suit before you buy it.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: become unusable through long use, or make tired
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: These boots are worn out after a year of walking.

collocations.meaning: a painting, sculpture, etc., of high quality
art created in the modern era
a room or building where works of art are shown
a public display of works of art
dress yourself in clothing
put on clothing to test the fit
have clothing on your body
remove clothing from your body
a cord or rope on which wet clothes are hung to dry

one's finest or most formal outfits

a blend or mixture of
say something nice to someone to show admiration
express praise or admiration to someone for something

such as; as an instance

no longer popular or trendy

an event where models show new clothing designs

collocations.collocation: have (/ get) a new hairdo (/ hairstyle)
collocations.meaning: obtain a new hair design or cut
get one's hair cut by a barber or stylist
relax and enjoy yourself without worrying
put cosmetics on one's face
remove cosmetics from one's face
conform to a design or sequence of events
a pattern of squares of different colors
a pattern of parallel bands or lines
a simple pattern with no design or stripes
trendy, fashionable, or in a luxurious way
no longer fashionable
do something in an impressive or grand manner
be elegant or fashionable
have good/bad judgment in choosing things like food, clothes, etc.
polite/impolite or socially acceptable/unacceptable
be likely to behave in a particular way
a general direction in which something is developing
do or wear what is currently popular
start a fashion that others copy

wordPatterns.meaning: tell someone what they should do
wordPatterns.example: EX: The doctor advised him to get more exercise.

wordPatterns.meaning: inform someone about a fact or advice
wordPatterns.example: EX: We advise passengers that trains may be delayed.

wordPatterns.meaning: give someone advice about a subject
wordPatterns.example: EX: She advises clients on their financial investments.

wordPatterns.meaning: recommend that someone should not do something
wordPatterns.example: EX: I would advise against buying that car.

wordPatterns.meaning: worried or concerned about a situation or action
wordPatterns.example: EX: He is anxious about the interview tomorrow.

wordPatterns.meaning: wanting to do something very much
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is anxious to finish the project on time.

wordPatterns.meaning: express disapproval of someone because of something
wordPatterns.example: EX: The mayor was criticized for his comments.

wordPatterns.meaning: demand something forcefully
wordPatterns.example: EX: He insisted on paying for the meal.

wordPatterns.meaning: state a demand or opinion firmly
wordPatterns.example: EX: She insisted that she was innocent.

wordPatterns.meaning: a large or sufficient amount of something
wordPatterns.example: EX: There is plenty of food in the fridge.

wordPatterns.meaning: a much larger remaining quantity of something
wordPatterns.example: EX: Don't worry, there are plenty more glasses in the cupboard.

wordPatterns.meaning: many things to perform
wordPatterns.example: EX: We have plenty to do before the guests arrive.

wordPatterns.meaning: get someone or something ready for an event
wordPatterns.example: EX: She prepared her son for the exams.

wordPatterns.meaning: get ready to perform an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: They prepared to leave the house.

wordPatterns.meaning: feeling pleased and satisfied about something or someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is very proud of her daughter's achievements.

wordPatterns.meaning: feeling pleased to perform an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: I am proud to represent my school.

wordPatterns.meaning: mention or direct attention to something
wordPatterns.example: EX: Please refer to the dictionary for the definition.

wordPatterns.meaning: send someone to a person or place for help or information
wordPatterns.example: EX: The doctor referred me to a specialist.

wordPatterns.meaning: appear to be
wordPatterns.example: EX: They seem to be very happy together.

wordPatterns.meaning: it appears that
wordPatterns.example: EX: It seems that the meeting has been cancelled.

wordPatterns.meaning: look at someone or something with eyes wide open for a long time
wordPatterns.example: EX: Don't stare at people, it's rude.

wordPatterns.meaning: employ something for a purpose or task
wordPatterns.example: EX: She uses a laptop for writing reports.

wordPatterns.meaning: employ something to perform an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: He used a hammer to fix the fence.

wordPatterns.meaning: helping to do something or achieve a goal
wordPatterns.example: EX: This knife is useful for cutting vegetables.

wordPatterns.meaning: helping or being of service to someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: The advice was very useful to me.

wordFormation.meaning: advertise: publish information to sell something / advertisement: a notice or picture that promotes a product / advertising: the business of designing promotions / advertiser: a person or company that advertises
wordFormation.example:
EX: The company decided to advertise their new product online.
EX: I saw an advertisement for a new smartphone.
EX: He works in the advertising industry.
EX: The newspaper relies on local advertisers.

wordFormation.meaning: attract: draw interest or pull towards / attraction: a place or feature that draws people / (un)attractive: (not) pleasing or appealing to look at
wordFormation.example:
EX: The show attracted a large audience.
EX: The Eiffel Tower is a popular tourist attraction.
EX: The new model is very attractive, but the old one was unattractive.

wordFormation.meaning: beauty: the quality of being pleasing to the senses / beautiful: very pretty or pleasing to look at
wordFormation.example:
EX: We admired the natural beauty of the landscape.
EX: She wore a beautiful dress to the wedding.

wordFormation.meaning: decide: make a choice or judgment / (in)decisive: (not) able to make decisions quickly and confidently / undecided: not yet having made a decision or resolved
wordFormation.example:
EX: She decided to study design.
EX: A decisive manager is crucial, while indecisive leaders cause delays.
EX: She remains undecided about which dress to buy.

wordFormation.meaning: desire: want something strongly / (un)desirable: (not) worth having or wanting
wordFormation.example:
EX: He has a strong desire to succeed.
EX: A quiet neighborhood is highly desirable, while pollution is undesirable.

wordFormation.meaning: enthuse: express excitement or praise / (un)enthusiastic: showing (a lack of) intense interest or excitement
wordFormation.example:
EX: He enthused about the performance.
EX: The fans were enthusiastic, but the response from critics was unenthusiastic.

wordFormation.meaning: expect: think that something will happen / expectation: a belief about the future / expectancy: the state of hoping or expecting something / expectance: expectation / (un)expected: (not) anticipated or planned
wordFormation.example:
EX: We expect the train to arrive on time.
EX: The show did not meet our high expectations.
EX: Life expectancy has increased over the years.
EX: The announcement was completely unexpected.

wordFormation.meaning: succeed: achieve a goal or follow after / (un)successful: (not) achieving the desired results / success: the achievement of an aim or purpose
wordFormation.example:
EX: He succeeded in passing the exam, and later succeeded his father as manager.
EX: The attempt was successful, unlike their unsuccessful project last year.
EX: Hard work is the key to success.

wordFormation.meaning: like: similar to / unlike: different from / likeness: similarity / dislike: find unpleasant / alike: similar / liking: a feeling of regard or fondness / likeable: pleasant and easy to like
wordFormation.example:
EX: She is like her sister in many ways, unlike their brother.
EX: I can see a strong likeness between them.
EX: We discussed our likes and dislikes.
EX: The twins look alike, and great minds think alike.
EX: She has a liking for fine art.
EX: He is an exceptionally likeable person.

wordFormation.meaning: produce: make or create / product: something that is made / producer: a person or company that makes goods / production: the process of making / productive: making or capable of making large amounts
wordFormation.example:
EX: The factory produces cars.
EX: The company released a new product.
EX: He is a well-known film producer.
EX: Agricultural production has increased.
EX: The meeting was highly productive, unlike the unproductive one yesterday.

wordFormation.meaning: similar: like but not identical / dissimilar: different / similarity: the state of being similar
wordFormation.example:
EX: The two designs are similar, but completely dissimilar to the third one.
EX: I found many similarities between their styles.

wordFormation.meaning: style: a way of doing or designing something / stylish: fashionable and elegant / stylishness: the quality of being stylish / stylishly: in a fashionable manner
wordFormation.example:
EX: She has a unique style of painting.
EX: He looked very stylish in his new suit, unlike his unstylish friend.
EX: The interior design has great stylishness.
EX: She was stylishly dressed for the party.

wordFormation.meaning: use: employing something / usage: the way something is used / user: person who uses / useful: of practical use / useless: of no practical use / disused: no longer used / usable: fit to be used
wordFormation.example:
EX: I found a use for this old box.
EX: The book covers English usage.
EX: The software is simple for the user.
EX: This tool is extremely useful, unlike that useless one.
EX: They converted a disused railway line.
EX: The format is usable on all devices.

wordFormation.meaning: fashion: popular style of clothing / fashionable: currently popular / unfashionable: not popular
wordFormation.example:
EX: She works in the fashion industry.
EX: It is currently fashionable to wear vintage clothes, but flared jeans are unfashionable.
